title: Toy English intensifier codebook
description: Toy infrastructure exemplar for a codebook-as-program. It is not an empirically validated codebook.

annotation_target.definition: A candidate token is labelled as an intensifier when its local function is to scale the degree of a gradable expression.
inclusion_criteria: The candidate has a local degree-scaling relation to a gradable adjective, adverb, verb phrase, or quantificational expression.
The candidate's contribution is part of the described clause rather than a metalinguistic aside.
exclusion_criteria: Focus particles whose main contribution is exhaustivity or contrast rather than degree scaling.
Discourse markers whose main contribution is stance or turn management.

projection_target.supported_question: Can a fixed annotation procedure support within-register frequency comparisons of degree-intensifier candidates?
projection_target.target_description: The intended downstream inference is a register-internal frequency comparison after validation, not a claim about speaker cognition or semantic representation.

exclusions: No corpus, historical, dialectal, or psycholinguistic coverage is claimed for this exemplar.

input_assumptions: Each candidate token is supplied with enough local context to identify the expression it may modify.
The input record identifies the candidate string and its sentence or clause context.
The annotator may abstain when the local function cannot be resolved.

labels.name: Intensifier
labels.definition: The candidate locally scales the degree of a gradable expression.
positive_cues: The candidate answers a degree question such as how much or to what extent.
Removing the candidate changes degree without changing the propositional frame.
negative_cues: The candidate selects a focus alternative set without degree scaling.
The candidate is part of an idiom whose contribution is not locally recoverable.

examples.source: constructed toy example; not corpus evidence
examples.rationale: Very scales the gradable adjective clear.

anti_examples.source: constructed toy example; not corpus evidence
anti_examples.rationale: Only marks focus/exhaustivity rather than degree.

labels.name: Not intensifier
labels.definition: The candidate does not locally scale the degree of a gradable expression.
positive_cues: The candidate marks focus, discourse stance, polarity, or another non-degree relation.
The candidate is quoted, mentioned, or otherwise outside ordinary clause interpretation.
negative_cues: The candidate directly modifies a gradable expression.
The candidate can be paraphrased as increasing or decreasing degree.

examples.source: constructed toy example; not corpus evidence
examples.rationale: Only does not scale the degree of clear.

anti_examples.source: constructed toy example; not corpus evidence
anti_examples.rationale: Extremely scales the gradable adjective clear.

labels.name: Abstain
labels.definition: The local evidence is insufficient for the declared decision rules.
positive_cues: The candidate's scope cannot be recovered from the supplied context.
The candidate sits in a quotation, repair, or fragment where ordinary clause function is unclear.
negative_cues: The candidate has a clear local degree-scaling target.
The candidate clearly has a non-degree function.

decision_rules.title: Find the degree target first
decision_rules.rule: Assign intensifier only if the candidate has a local gradable target in the supplied context.

decision_rules.title: Do not treat focus alone as intensification
decision_rules.rule: If the candidate's main contribution is focus, contrast, or exhaustivity and no independent degree target is present, assign not-intensifier.

decision_rules.title: Abstain when scope is unrecoverable
decision_rules.rule: If the candidate's local scope or target cannot be recovered from the supplied context, assign abstain rather than guessing.

hard_cases.description: Candidate may plausibly be either degree-scaling or focus-marking.
hard_cases.default_action: Apply the degree-target-first rule; abstain if no local target can be identified.

hard_cases.description: Candidate appears inside quoted or mentioned language.
hard_cases.default_action: Annotate only if the quoted material is within the declared annotation target; otherwise abstain.

uncertainty.interpretation: Confidence records decision stability under the codebook, not psychological certainty.

instrument_assumptions: The instrument can return JSON matching the output schema.
The instrument receives the same codebook text and candidate context for each run.
The instrument is treated as a measurement aid whose outputs require validation.
forbidden_dependencies: Do not rely on model-specific hidden memory.
Do not infer corpus frequencies from model priors.
Do not use examples as empirical evidence.

target_metrics: human agreement on adjudicated gold sample
effect of abstention on downstream frequency estimates

supported_inferences.claim: After validation, labels may support within-register frequency comparisons of degree-intensifier candidates.
supported_inferences.scope: The same register, sampling frame, candidate extraction procedure, and codebook version.
supported_inferences.evidence_required: Gold adjudication plus cross-instrument agreement and stable disagreement classes.

unsupported_inferences: No claim about individual speaker cognition.
No claim about all English registers.
No claim about diachronic change without a separate measurement-drift check.
scope_conditions: Candidate extraction must be held fixed.
The same codebook version must be used across compared samples.
Abstentions must be reported rather than silently dropped.
demotion_triggers: Cross-instrument disagreement concentrates in a single label without a rule-level explanation.
Human adjudicators cannot apply the degree-target-first rule consistently.
Abstention rates differ sharply across registers being compared.

provenance.notes: Toy exemplar for infrastructure testing only.
